actions.text: ### Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Test partnership structure agreed**: Lensa will pay Careerspan on CPC basis (15¢ per click) with $2,500 starting budget per feed. Careerspan will distribute Lensa's 25M jobs to our talent network and send traffic back to Lensa for user registration. This matters because it validates our distribution channel model and provides revenue while solving our job sourcing challenges (cleaner JD data vs. current scraping). Rationale: Lensa sees Careerspan's high-quality, engaged users as valuable "fresh job seekers" in an industry where users bounce between platforms.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Technical integration via Appcast**: Lensa will set up Careerspan as master publisher in Appcast to receive XML feeds or API access. Start with product-focused jobs (Boston/SF/NY, posted ≤3 days ago), then expand to engineering/growth roles within 3 months. This matters because it gives us access to clean, structured job data across all content types (organic, syndicated, direct agency) without scraping overhead. Rationale: Appcast is industry standard for job distribution, and Lensa can segment feeds by job category to match our current product-role focus.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Performance metrics and scaling path**: Two analysts assigned (one for direct content, one for lead gen). They'll monitor user interaction with job alerts and ROI, then scale up/down budgets accordingly. Initial test targets 70-80 jobs/week to 700-1000 activated Careerspan users (3-5% monthly reactivation rate, 75-80% open rate, 15-16% CTR). This matters because it establishes clear success criteria and scaling mechanism - if engagement performs, budgets increase. Rationale: Lensa values quality over volume given Careerspan's superior engagement metrics vs. typical job board traffic.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ### Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Lensa is a major job board aggregator (25M jobs) with US-only operations but Hungary-based dev team. They operate in a "little incestuous" industry where most partners already work with each other, distributing jobs via XML/Appcast infrastructure. Mai Flynn leads user acquisition and is explicitly looking to "tap into fresh job seekers" outside the typical job board circulation.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Careerspan's positioning resonated strongly: high-engagement users (75-80% open, 15-16% CTR), rich behavioral data beyond resumes, community-embedded distribution model. Our matching system's quality-over-volume approach was acknowledged as potentially lower CPC volume but higher-value traffic. Mai accepted this tradeoff explicitly: "nice to tap into just fresh job seekers."
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Key pain point for Careerspan: current job scraping requires "buttload of effort" to clean JDs. Lensa's structured feeds solve this immediately while also providing revenue channel. Win-win: we get clean job data + revenue, they get high-quality users.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Industry context: Job board industry has high user churn ("users just bounce from one platform to the next"), making new distribution channels valuable. Lensa is willing to test with new partners despite Careerspan being small/new because of engagement quality signals.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window
